notes: SMN1 encodes survival motor neuron protein; homozygous loss causes SMA
C-to-T transition in SMN2 exon 7 causes ~90% exon skipping (Lorson et al. 1999)
Loss of SMN protein leads to motor neuron degeneration in SMA (Lefebvre et al. 1997)
STMN2 restoration rescues motor neuron axonal defects (Melamed et al. 2019 + follow-up)
STMN2 as downstream target of TDP-43, neuroprotection in motor neurons
PLS3 as protective modifier of SMA severity (Oprea et al. 2008)
NCALD suppression rescues SMA in zebrafish model (Riessland et al. 2017)
Ubiquitin pathway dysregulation in SMA motor neurons (Wishart et al. 2014)
mTOR hyperactivation in SMA motor neurons (Kye et al. 2014, Hensel et al.)
SMA Type I: severe muscle weakness from motor neuron loss
NMJ defects are earliest pathological feature of SMA (Kariya et al. 2008)
Respiratory insufficiency is leading cause of mortality in SMA Type I
